summary: Verified and improved accessibility for the US-841 start/resume UI. The resume prompt now places Continue before start-new choices in keyboard order, start-new choices are exposed as a labelled group, start buttons have distinct accessible names, and start/resume controls have visible keyboard focus styling.

tests_run.details: 9 test files passed, 72 tests passed, production build passed

verification_environment.local_server: http://127.0.0.1:5331/ and http://localhost:5331/ via npm run dev

evidence_matrix.scenario: Start screen exposes supported bullet-size buttons as native buttons with distinct accessible names and a labelled group.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: Browser evidence and source review of src/ui/render.ts
evidence_matrix.limitations: Automated browser Tab keypress did not provide reliable focus movement in this in-app browser runtime; DOM order and native control semantics were verified instead.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Resume prompt keyboard order prioritizes Continue before destructive/replacement start-new choices.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: Browser evidence and npm run test -- src/ui/controller.test.ts
evidence_matrix.limitations: Keyboard order was verified by DOM order of native focusable buttons.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Start/resume controls expose visible keyboard focus styling.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: Browser stylesheet evidence and source review of src/styles.css
evidence_matrix.limitations: Visual screenshots are owned by MZ-106.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: Browser DOM evidence, source review, regression test, and Rawls review
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: No axe dependency is present in the project; used native browser/source evidence.
quality_gate_matrix.accepted_risk: No blocking accessibility risk for start/resume flow.

non_blocking_notes: Suggested role="group" on the labelled start-actions div; implemented before closing task.
